info.title: RailGadi API
info.description:
Live train tracking and Indian Railways data API.

**Base URL:** `https://2fe4m4jegh2fuhgkk3u7v4sqv40qpago.lambda-url.ap-south-1.on.aws`

All responses are wrapped in a standard envelope:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { ... },
  "meta": {
    "traceId": "uuid",
    "timestamp": "2026-03-17T09:01:49+05:30",
    "executionTime": 183,
    "source": "database | hybrid | external"
  }
}
```

tags.name: Trains
tags.description: Train schedules, routes, and live tracking

tags.name: Live Map
tags.description: Real-time positions of all running trains

tags.name: Lookup
tags.description: Fast key-value lookups for stations and trains

tags.name: Legacy
tags.description: Legacy API endpoints for backward compatibility

get.description: Returns static train info with full schedule. Use `haltsOnly=true` to skip pass-through stops.

parameters.name: haltsOnly
parameters.description: Return only halting stops (excludes pass-through). Default: `false`

get.description:
Returns the route geometry for a train.

**Format options (`format` param):**
- `geojson` (default) — GeoJSON `LineString` feature. Ready for Leaflet, MapLibre, QGIS, geojson.io.
- `polyline` — [Google encoded polyline](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm) (precision 5). Smallest payload (~8KB avg). Decode with any standard polyline library.
- `coordinates` — Raw `[[lat, lng], ...]` array. For custom renderers.

**Stops (`stops` param):**
Pass `stops=true` to include all station stops (code, name, lat, lng, sequence) alongside the geometry. Useful for drawing station markers on the route in a single request.

Returns `null` geometry if the route has not been generated yet (run `route:generate` pipeline step).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format for the geometry. Default: `geojson`

parameters.name: stops
parameters.description: Include station stops (code, name, lat, lng) alongside the geometry. Default: `false`

get.description:
Real-time position, delay, and per-stop status for a train journey.

- `date` is optional — omit it and the API auto-detects the most recent journey date based on the train's running days and current IST time.
- `currentLocation` includes GPS `coordinates` when live position data is available from external sources.
- `status` can be `running`, `not-started`, `completed`, or `cancelled`.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Journey start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Omit to auto-detect.

parameters.name: haltsOnly
parameters.description: Return only halting stops in the route array. Default: `false`

parameters.name: includeCoordinates
parameters.description: Include latitude and longitude for each station in the route array. Default: `false`

get.description:
Find all trains running between two stations, with optional date filtering and live status.

**Date filtering (`date` param):**
Trains run on specific weekdays. A train that departs its source on Saturday may only reach an intermediate station (e.g. UJN) on Sunday. The `date` param filters by the date the train is at the `from` station — not the source departure date.

Internally: `sourceDepDate = date − (fromDay − 1)`, then the source departure weekday is checked against the train's running days bitmap.

- Omit `date` → all trains returned regardless of day (useful for showing a full timetable)
- Pass `date` → only trains that actually run on that date at the `from` station
- Returns 404 if no trains run on the given date

**Live status (`live` param):**
- `live=false` (default) — static schedule only, very fast
- `live=true` — enriches each train with a `live` object at the `from` station:
  - `at-station` — train is currently at the station
  - `upcoming` — train is approaching
  - `departed` — train has left
  - `scheduled` — no live data yet
  - `not-running` — train not running (future date, cancelled, or no data)

**Future dates with `live=true`:** External data sources are not queried. Every train gets `{ "type": "not-running", "startDate": "..." }` immediately.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Filter trains by the date they are at the `from` station (YYYY-MM-DD). Accounts for multi-day journeys — a train departing its source on Saturday may arrive at an intermediate station on Sunday, so passing Sunday here will correctly include it. Omit to return all trains regardless of running day.

parameters.name: live
parameters.description: Enrich each train with live status at the `from` station. Default: `false`. When `true`, queries live data records for trains that could currently be running (source departed today or yesterday). For future dates, returns `not-running` for all trains without querying external sources.

404.description: No trains found between the given stations (or none run on the requested date)

error.message: No trains found between UJN and INDB

noTrainsOnDate.summary: No trains on the requested date

error.message: No trains found between UJN and INDB on 2026-03-15

get.description: All trains that stop at a station with scheduled times. Use `includeIntermediate=true` to also include pass-through trains.

Live arrival/departure board — similar to platform displays.

**Time window:** `[now − 4h, now + hoursAhead]`

Each train entry includes a `live` object with one of:
- `at-station` — train is currently at the station (includes `expectedDepartureTime`)
- `upcoming` — train is approaching (includes `expectedArrivalTime`)
- `departed` — train has left (includes `departedAt`)
- `scheduled` — no live data yet, showing scheduled time

**Buffer logic:** Trains whose scheduled time is in the past are only shown if live data confirms they are still `at-station` or `upcoming`. Already-departed or no-data trains in the buffer window are dropped.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Hours ahead to look for trains. Allowed: `2`, `4`, `6`, `8`. Default: `4`

get.description:
Enhanced live map showing real-time positions of all running trains.

Returns an array of trains currently en route with:
- Current station (last departed) and next station
- Interpolated GPS coordinates between stations
- Minutes since departure from source
- Distance covered and remaining
- Journey day information

The response includes metadata about:
- Total trains in the system
- Number of trains with live data records
- Coverage percentage
- Performance metrics (DB query time, external data fetch time, processing time)

Response is cached for 1 minute.

200.description: Live train positions with enhanced metadata

get.description: Returns a flat `{ code: name }` map for every station. Useful for client-side autocomplete. Response is cached.
Returns a flat `{ number: name }` map for every **active** train. Cancelled/inactive trains are excluded. Response is cached.

get.summary: Local trains for a specific city
get.description:
Returns all local trains for a specific city as a flat `{ number: name }` map.

**Local trains** are identified by having a non-empty `CITY` value in the database. Empty string in the `CITY` column indicates passenger trains (excluded from results).

**Supported cities:**
- Kolkata
- Hyderabad
- Mumbai
- Chennai

The `city` parameter is **required** and case-insensitive. Response is cached.

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: City name (case-insensitive). Must be one of: Kolkata, Hyderabad, Mumbai, Chennai

200.description: Local trains lookup map for the specified city

400.description: Validation error - missing or invalid city parameter

error.message: City parameter is required. Valid cities: Kolkata, Hyderabad, Mumbai, Chennai
city: City must be one of: Kolkata, Hyderabad, Mumbai, Chennai

get.description:
Backward compatibility layer preserving the old API structure for existing clients.

**Query parameters:**
- `journeyDate` (optional) — Journey start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. If omitted, auto-detects based on train's running days and current IST time.
- `dataType` (optional) — Data type to return. Default: `full`
  - `full` — Static train data + live data (if available)
  - `static` — Static train data only (schedule, route)
  - `live` — Live tracking data only from external sources
- `dataProvider` (optional) — Data provider preference. Default: `railradar`
  - `railradar` — Use RailRadar database
  - `NTES` — Use NTES (not currently implemented)
- `userId` (optional) — User ID for tracking/analytics

**Response format:**
Unlike the new API endpoints, this returns the legacy `TrainData` format directly without the standard envelope wrapper. The response structure varies based on `dataType`:

- `dataType=static` → Returns `{ train, route, metadata }`
- `dataType=live` → Returns live status object with current location and route updates
- `dataType=full` → Returns static data merged with live data when available

**Caching:**
- Static data: 24 hours
- Live/full data: 20 seconds

parameters.name: journeyDate
parameters.description: Journey start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Auto-detected if omitted.

parameters.name: dataType
parameters.description: Type of data to return. Default: `full`

200.description: Legacy train data (format varies by dataType parameter)

Meta.description: Response metadata present on every successful response

encodedPolyline.description: Present when format=polyline. Google encoded polyline (precision 5).

geojson.description: Present when format=geojson. GeoJSON LineString feature.

stops.description: Present when stops=true. Station stops along the route.

arrivalDay.description: Journey day for arrival (1 = day of departure)

speedToNextStationKmph.description: Static speed to next station in kmph

lastUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 +05:30 — last live data update time

sequence.description: null when train is on a diverted route not in DB

coordinates.description: GPS coordinates — present when live position data is available

distanceFromOriginKm.description: Distance traveled from train origin station in km (rounded to 0.1km)

distanceFromLastStationKm.description: Distance traveled from the last station in km (rounded to 0.1km) — used for accurate progress calculation between stations

actualArrival.description: ISO 8601 +05:30 — null if not yet arrived

actualDeparture.description: ISO 8601 +05:30 — null if not yet departed

speedToNextStationKmph.description: Static speed to next station in kmph

message.example: Train is diverted between UJN and INDB

TrainRouteGeometry.description: Route geometry in the requested format. Only one of encodedPolyline / geojson / coordinates will be present.

geojson.description: GeoJSON LineString Feature. Present when format=geojson.

day.description: Journey day number at from-station (1 = day of source departure)

distance.description: km between from and to stations

totalHaltsBetween.description: Halts between from and to (excluding endpoints)

live.description: Only present when `live=true` is passed. Null when no live data available.

LiveNotRunning.description: Train is not running today, cancelled, diverted, or no data available

window.description: IST time window used for the query

day.description: Journey day number at this station

live.description: Number of trains with live data records

departureMinutes.description: Scheduled departure from current station (minutes from midnight)

currentDay.description: Journey day number at current station

nextArrivalMinutes.description: Scheduled arrival at next station (minutes from midnight)

currDistance.description: km from source to current station

nextDistance.description: km from source to next station

LegacyTrainEnvelope.description: Legacy train data response envelope. Data structure varies based on dataType parameter.

message.example: Train data not available for this date.

liveData.description: Present when live data is available
